DISCLAIMER: I AM NOT AN ATTORNEY AND THIS IS ONLY THE DOCUMENT THAT WE USED. YOU SHOULD HAVE AN ATTORNEY REVIEW BEFORE USING THIS DOCUMENT. DISCLAIMER - [YOUR NAME], LLC IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY LIABILITY OF THE USE OF THIS DOCUMENT.



OPERATING AGREEMENT



OF



[YOURCOMPANY], LLC



	This Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of [YOURCOMPANY], LLC, a limited liability company organized pursuant to the Georgia Limited Liability Company Act, is entered into and is effective as of the Effective Date, by and among the Company with the unanimous consent of the Members.  



SECTION 1.	DEFINITIONS



	The terms and conditions used in this Agreement have the meanings set forth below, unless otherwise expressly provided herein.



	1.1	“Act” means the Georgia Limited Liability Act, and all amendments.



	1.2	“Agreement” means this Amended and Restated Operating Agreement, as originally executed and as it may be amended from time to time.



	1.3	“Articles of Organization” means the Articles of Organization of the Company filed with the Secretary of State of Georgia for the purpose of forming the Company, pursuant to the Act, as amended.



	1.4	“Bankrupt Member” means a Member who (a) has become the subject for an order for relief under the United States Bankruptcy Code, or (b) has initiated, either in an original proceeding or by way of answer in any state, insolvency receivership proceeding, an action for liquidation arrangements, composition, readjustment, dissolution or similar relief.



	1.5	“Capital Contribution” means any Member's contribution to the capital of the Company in cash, property, services rendered or a promissory note or other binding obligation to contribute cash or property or to render services.



	1.6	“Company” refers to [YOURCOMPANY], LLC, and any successor limited liability company.



	1.7	“Distribution” means any cash and other property paid by the Company to a Member in his, her or its capacity as a Member.



	1.8	“Entity” means any general partnership, limited partnership, limited liability company, corporation, joint venture, trust, business trust, cooperative or association, or any foreign trust or foreign business organization.



	1.9	“Fiscal Year” means the fiscal year of the Company, which shall be from January 1 to December 31 each year.



	1.10	“Manager” means one or more managers. References to the Manager in the singular or as him, her, it, itself, or other like references will also, where the context so requires, be deemed to include the plural or the masculine or feminine reference, as the case may be.



	1.11	“Member” means each of the parties who executes a counterpart of this Operating Agreement as a Member, and each of the parties who may subsequently become Members in accordance with Section 10. The term includes initial members, substituted members and additional members.



	1.12	“Membership Interest” means a Member's entire interest in the Company, including the (i) the percentage of any Distributions to which a Member is entitled, (ii) the percent of the property of the Company to which a Member is entitled upon liquidation of the Company, and (iii) the right to participate in the management or affairs of the Company, including the right to vote on, consent to or otherwise participate in any decision of the Members.



	1.13	“Person” means any association, corporation, stock company, estate, general partnership (including any Registered Limited Liability Partnership or Foreign Limited Liability Partnership), limited association, limited liability company (including a professional service limited liability company), foreign limited liability company, joint venture, limited partnership, natural person, real estate investment trust, business trust or other trust, custodian, nominee or other individual in its own or any representative capacity. In addition, the term means the heirs, executors, administrators, legal representatives, successors and assigns of that “Person” where the context so permits.



SECTION 2.	ORGANIZATION



	2.1	Formation.  The Company was organized on September 1, 2002 by executing and delivering Articles of Organization to the Secretary of State of Georgia in accordance with and pursuant to the Act. The Members adopted Amended and Restated Articles of Organization on _____________, which replaced the Articles of Organization filed on September 1, 2002.



	2.2	Agreement.  In consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which is acknowledged, the Members executing this Agreement agree to the terms and conditions of this Agreement as it may be from time to time amended according to its terms. The Members expressly intend that the Agreement be the sole source of agreement of the parties, and except to the extent that a provision of this Agreement is expressly prohibited or ineffective under the Act, even when inconsistent with or different from the provisions of the Act or any other law or rule. To the extent that any Agreement provision is prohibited or ineffective under the Act, the Agreement is considered amended to the smallest degree possible in order to make the Agreement effective under the Act. If the Act is subsequently amended or interpreted in such a way to make any formerly invalid provision of the Agreement valid, the provision will be considered to be valid from the effective date of the interpretation or amendment.



	2.3	Name.  The name of the Company is [YOURCOMPANY], LLC, and all business of the Company will be conducted under that name or any other trade name selected by the Managers, but in any case, only to the extent permitted by applicable law.



	2.4	Effective Date.  This Agreement is effective on _______________ (the “Effective Date”).



	2.5	Principal Place of Business.  The Company's principal place of business within the State of Georgia will be 3300 Highlands Parkway, Suite 260, Smyrna, Georgia, 30080. The Company may establish any other places of business as the Managers deem advisable.



	2.6	Registered Office and Registered Agent.  The Company's Registered Agent is [PARTNERS NAME],  its registered office is at 3300 Highlands Parkway, Suite 260, Smyrna, Georgia, 30080. The Registered Office and Registered Agent may be changed from time to time by filing the address of the new Registered Office and/or the name of the new Registered Agent with the Secretary of State of Georgia pursuant to the Act. 



	2.7	Term.  The Company's term is perpetual from the date of filing of the Articles of Organization with the Secretary of State, unless the Company is dissolved and its affairs wound up in accordance with the Act and this Agreement.



SECTION 3.	BUSINESS OF COMPANY



	3.1	Nature of Business.  The Company's purpose and business is:



		(a)	To accomplish any lawful business whatsoever.



		(b)	To exercise all other powers necessary to or reasonably connected with the Company's 	business that may be legally exercised by limited liability companies under the Act.



		(c)	To engage in all activities necessary, customary, or convenient, or incident to any of the 	foregoing.



	3.2	Other Business Activities.  The Company exists only for the purposes specified in Section 3.1 above and may not conduct any other business without the unanimous consent of the Members.  The authority granted to the Managers to bind the Company is limited to the actions necessary or advisable to further the Company’s business purpose.



SECTION 4.	MEMBERS



	4.1	Names and Addresses.  The names and addresses of the Members are set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement, which is attached hereto and incorporated herein by this reference.



	4.2	Additional Members.  In the event that a Person is subsequently admitted as a Member after the Effective Date of this Agreement in accordance with Section 10, that Person's name, address and Membership Interest must be added to Exhibit A.



	4.3	Membership Certificates.  Membership Interests in the Company will not be represented by certificates.  The Company shall maintain a current list of its Members and their respective Membership Interests.



SECTION 5.	MEMBER RIGHTS AND DUTIES



	5.1	Management Rights.  All Members are entitled to vote on any matter submitted to a vote of the Members. The following actions require the unanimous vote or written consent of all of the Members:



		(a)	Any amendment to the Articles of Organization.



(b)	Any amendment to this Agreement.



		(c)	The merger of the Company with another limited liability company or Entity.



		(d)	Incurring indebtedness by the Company other than in the ordinary course of business.



(e)	Authorizing a transaction involving an actual or potential conflict of interest between a Member and the Company.



(f)	Changing the nature of the business of the Company.



(g)	Admitting a Person as a Member.



(h)	The approval of a sale, lease, exchange or other disposition of all or substantially all of the Company's assets.



(i)	Commencing a voluntary petition for bankruptcy for the Company.



	5.2	Voting.  Whenever a vote of the Member is permitted or required by this Agreement, the Act, or other applicable law, each Member shall have the right to vote in proportion to such Member’s Membership Interests in the Company.  In the case of approvals to withdrawal where consent of the remaining Members is required the withdrawing Member will not be considered a Member entitled to vote. 



	5.3	Limitation of Liability.  Members are not liable for any debts, obligations or liabilities of the Company or each other, whether arising in tort, contract or otherwise, solely by reason of being a Member. 



	5.4	Indemnification.  The Company will indemnify the Members, Managers, and agents for all costs, losses, liabilities and damages paid or accrued by the Member, Manager or agent in connection with the Company's business, to the fullest extent provided or allowed by the laws of Georgia.



	5.5	Books and Records.  The Managers will maintain and preserve during the Company's term all accounts, books, minutes of Member meetings, and all other relevant Company documents. On reasonable request, each Member has the right, during ordinary business hours and at the Company's principal place of business, to inspect and copy Company documents at the requesting Member's expense.



	5.6	Priority and Return of Capital.  Except as expressly provided in Sections 7 or 8, no Member has priority over any other Member, whether for the return of a Capital Contribution or a Distribution; provided, however, that this Section does not apply to loans or other indebtedness (as distinguished from a Capital Contribution) made by a Member to the Company.



	5.7	Liability to Company.  A Member who rightfully receives the return of any portion of a Capital Contribution is liable to the Company only to the extent now or subsequently provided by the Act. A Member who receives a Distribution made by the Company in violation of this Agreement, or made when the Company's liabilities exceed its assets (after giving effect to the Distribution) is liable to the Company for the amount of the Distribution for a period of four (4) years after the Distribution.



	5.8	Representations and Warranties.  Each Member, and in the case of an organization, the person(s) executing the Agreement on the organization's behalf, represents and warrants to the Company and each other Member and Manager that: (a) if that Member is an organization, it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the law of its state of organization, and has full organizational power to execute and agree to the Agreement to perform its obligations under the Agreement; (b) the Member is acquiring its interest in the Company for the Member's own account as an investment and without intent to distribute the interest; and (c) the Member acknowledges that the interests have not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933 or any state securities laws, and may not be resold or transferred by the Member without appropriate registration or the availability of an exemption from those requirements.



5.9	Fiduciary Duties; Conflicts of Interest.



	(a)	Each Member owes the fiduciary duties of care and loyalty to the Company and the other Members, and must discharge these duties and exercise the Member’s rights in the Company consistently with the obligation of good faith and fair dealing.  Accordingly, a Member may not enter into transactions that may be considered competitive with, or into a business opportunity that may be beneficial to, the Company, without the written consent of all other Members.  



	(b)	A Member does not violate a duty or obligation to the Company merely because the Member's conduct furthers the member's own interest. A Member may lend money to and transact other business with the Company. The rights and obligations of a Member who lends money to or transacts business with the Company are the same as those of a non-Member, subject to other applicable law. No transaction with the Company will be voidable solely because a Member has a direct or indirect interest in the transaction if either (i) the transaction is fair to the Company, or (ii) disinterested Members, knowing the material facts of the transaction and the Member's interest, authorize, approve or ratify the transaction.





SECTION 6.	MANAGEMENT OF THE COMPANY



	6.1	Managers; Fiduciaries.  The business and affairs of the Company will be managed by its Managers. The Managers shall direct, manage and control the Company's business to the best of their ability and using their best business judgment. Except for situations in which Member approval is expressly required by this Operating Agreement or by nonwaivable provisions of law, the Managers have full authority and discretion to manage and control the Company's business, affairs and properties, to make all decisions regarding those matters, and to perform any and all other acts or activities customary or incident to the management of the Company's business.  Each Manager owes the fiduciary duties of care and loyalty to the Company and to the Members, and must discharge these duties and exercise the Manager's rights in the Company consistently with the obligation of good faith and fair dealing.



	6.2	Number, Tenure and Qualifications.  The Company will have two (2) Managers. Each of the individuals listed on Exhibit B, which is attached hereto and incorporated herein by this reference, will initially serve as the Managers. The number of Managers of the Company may be amended from time to time by the vote or written consent of Members holding a majority of the Membership Interests.  Each Manager shall hold office until a successor has been elected and qualified. 



	6.3	Authority of Managers.  Each Manager shall have the authority, without the consent or approval of any other Manager, to bind the Company where the total amount of liability for any such transaction, or series of related transactions, is no more than $5,000.00. For all other transactions, the Managers, in exercising their authority, shall act as a group and no Manager shall have the authority to act on behalf of or bind the Company unless authorized by a unanimous vote of the Managers.  In addition, the following specific actions shall require a unanimous vote of the Managers:



	(a)	Purchase, lease or otherwise acquire from, or sell, lease or otherwise dispose of, to any person any Company property.



	(b)	Open bank accounts and otherwise invest the Company's funds.



	(c)	Borrow money for the Company from banks or other lending institutions, or to otherwise incur indebtedness on behalf of the Company or to hypothecate, encumber or grant security interests in the Company's assets. 



	(d)	Purchase insurance on the Company's business and assets.



	(e)	Commence lawsuits and other proceedings.



	(f)	Enter into any agreement, instrument or other writing.



	(g)	Retain accountants, attorneys or other agents.



	6.4	Binding Authority.  Unless authorized to do so by a majority vote of the Managers, no attorney-in-fact, employee or other agent of the Company has any power or authority to bind the Company in any way, to pledge its credit or to render it pecuniarily liable for any purpose. No Member has any power or authority to bind the Company unless the Member has been authorized by the Managers to act as an agent of the Company in accordance with the previous sentence.



	6.5	Liability for Certain Acts.  Each Manager must perform his or her duties as Manager in good faith, in a manner he or she reasonably believes to be in the best interest of the Company, and with such care as an ordinarily prudent person in a like position would use under similar circumstances. A Manager who so performs the duties as Manager does not have any liability by reason of being or having been a Manager of the Company. The Manager does not, in any way, guarantee the return of the Members' Capital Contributions or a profit for the Members from the Company operations. The Manager is not liable to the Company or to any Member for any loss or damage sustained by the Company or any Member, unless the loss or damage was the result of fraud, deceit, gross negligence, willful misconduct, or a wrongful taking by the Manager.



	6.6	Other Interests; Duty to Company.  The Managers are not required to manage the Company as their sole and exclusive function and they may have other business interests and may engage in other activities in addition to those relating to the Company; provided, however, that such other business interests and activities may not be competitive with the Company, cause a conflict of interest between the Manager and the Company, or cause a Manager to breach a fiduciary or other duty or obligation to the Company unless the Manager has obtained the consent of a majority of the disinterested Members. Neither the Company nor any Member shall have any right pursuant to this Agreement to share or participate in the other business interests or activities of the Managers or in the income or proceeds derived from them unless such other business interests or activities violate this Section 6.6. 



	6.7	Indemnification.  The Company must indemnify and hold harmless the Managers from and against all claims and demands to the maximum extent permitted under the Act.



	6.8	Resignation.  Any Manager may resign at any time by giving written notice to the Company, with a copy to each Member. The resignation of any Manager takes effect on receipt of notice by the Company or at any later time specified in the notice. Unless otherwise specified in the notice, the acceptance of the resignation is not necessary to make it effective. The resignation of the Manager who is also a Member does not affect the Manager's rights as a Member and does not constitute a withdrawal of the Member.



	6.9	Removal.  Any Manager may be removed or replaced with or without cause by the vote of Members who hold a majority of the Membership Interests. The removal of a Manager who is also a Member will not affect the Manager's rights as a Member and will not constitute a withdrawal of the Member.



	6.10	Vacancies.  Any vacancy occurring for any reason in the number of Managers may be filled by the vote or written consent of Members holding a majority of the Membership Interests. A Manager elected to fill a vacancy is elected for the unexpired term of the Manager's predecessor in office and holds office until the expiration of the term and until the Manager's successor has been elected and qualified. A Manager chosen to fill a position resulting from an increase in the number of Managers holds office until the next meeting of Members and until a successor has been elected and qualified.



	6.11	Salaries.  The salaries and other compensation of the Managers will be fixed from time to time by the vote or written consent of at least a majority of the Members. No Manager is prevented from receiving such a salary or other compensation because the Manager is also a Member.



SECTION 7.	CONTRIBUTIONS AND CAPITAL CONTRIBUTIONS



	7.1	Capital Contributions.  The Managers shall maintain a record of the Capital Contributions of each Member on the books and records of the Company.



	7.2	Additional Contributions.  Except for the initial Capital Contribution made by a Member in exchange for such Member’s Membership Interest, no Member is required to make any Capital Contributions. To the extent unanimously approved by the Managers, from time to time, the Members may be permitted to make additional Capital Contributions if and to the extent they so desire, and if the Managers determine that those additional Capital Contributions are necessary or appropriate in connection with the conduct of the Company's business (including without limitation, expansion or diversification). In such event, the Members may (but are not obligated) to participate in those additional Capital Contributions on a pro rata basis in accordance with their Membership Interests.  Following any such Capital Contributions, the Membership Interests of the Members will be adjusted to accurately reflect the total Capital Contributions of the Members after such additional Capital Contributions.



	7.3	Withdrawal or Reduction of Capital Contributions.  A Member will not receive from the Company any portion of his or her Capital Contribution until all indebtedness and liabilities of the Company, except any indebtedness, liabilities and obligations to Members on account of their Capital Contributions, have been paid or there remains property of the Company, in the sole discretion of the Managers, sufficient to pay them. No distributions will be made from the Capital Contributions of a Member without the consent of Members owning a majority of the Membership Interests in the Company. A Member, irrespective of the nature of the Capital Contribution of that Member, has only the right to demand and receive cash in return for the Capital Contribution.



SECTION 8.	ALLOCATIONS AND DISTRIBUTIONS.



	8.1	Distributions.  Except for a Distribution on dissolution in accordance with Section 15.2, the Managers may, from time to time, in the Managers' discretion, make Distributions to the Members.  All such distributions shall be made in accordance with the Membership Interest of the Members.



	8.2	Offset.  The Company may offset all amounts owing to the Company by a Member against any Distribution to be made to the Member.



	8.3	Limitation on Distributions.  No Distribution will be declared and paid unless, after such Distribution is made, the assets of the Company are in excess of all liabilities of the Company and the Company is not insolvent under the Act.



	8.4	Interest on and Return of Capital Contributions.  No Member is entitled to interest on his or her Capital Contribution, or to a return of his, her, or its Capital Contribution, except as specifically set forth in this Agreement.



SECTION 9.	TAXES



	9.1	Tax Returns.  The Managers must cause to be prepared and filed all necessary federal and state income tax returns for the Company. Copies of such returns or pertinent information from them, will be furnished to the Members within a reasonable time after the end of the Company's Fiscal Year. Each Member must furnish to the Managers all pertinent information in its possession relating to Company operations that is necessary to enable the Company's income tax returns to be prepared and filed.



	9.2	Tax Elections.  Company will make such tax elections that the Managers deem appropriate and in the best interests of the Members.



SECTION 10.	ADMISSION ADDITIONAL MEMBERS



	Any Person acceptable to the Members by their unanimous vote may become a Member of the Company, subject to the terms and conditions of this Operating Agreement. In such event, the Managers shall issue Membership Interests to such Person for such consideration as the Members determine by their unanimous vote.  Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, no Members shall be required to vote in favor of admitting any Person as a Member, and a Member may, in his or her sole and absolute discretion, withhold his or her consent to a Person being admitted as a Member for any reason or no reason at all.





SECTION 11.  DEADLOCK IN THE MANAGEMENT AND AFFAIRS OF THE COMAPNY



11.1	Procedure.  If the Members or Managers are deadlocked in the management of the business or financial affairs of the Company and the Members or Managers are unable to break the deadlock for a period of 30 days, any Member (the “Initiating Member”) may, at any time before the deadlock is broken, submit the disputed matter to Greg Chevalier, the Company’s business advisor, to break the tie and decide the disputed issue. In such event, the Managers of the Company shall immediately provide Mr. Chevalier with full and unrestricted access such books, records, and other information possessed by the Company that Mr. Chevalier deems appropriate to enable him to review all relevant facts and cast his vote to break the deadlock. In casting his vote, Mr. Chevalier shall seek to further the business interests of the Company.  



11.2	Unavailability of Mr. Chevailer.  In the event Mr. Chevalier is unavailable or for any reason cannot perform the tie-breaking functions described in this Section 11, then the Members shall select a certified public accountant to perform such functions in accordance with the selection procedures described in Section 14.2(c) relating to determining Current Value, except that the term Disabled Member shall be replaced with Initiating Member.  



11.3  Applicability of Section 11.  The tie breaking mechanism described in this Section 11 does not apply to deadlocks regarding the sale, acquisition, or merger of the Company. See Section 12 for resolution of a deadlock involving the sale, acquisition, or merger of the Company.



SECTION 12.  CROSS PURCHASE



12.1 	Cross Offer Notice.  If the Members are deadlocked in regards to a sale, acquisition, or merger of the Company, or sale of substantially all of the assets of the Company, for which a bona fide written offer has been received, and the Members are unable to break the deadlock for a period of 30 days after the receipt of such offer, any Member (the “Initiating Member”) may, at any time before the deadlock is broken, give a notice (the “Cross Offer Notice”) to any other Member of the Initiating Member’s intent to either sell all of the Initiating Member’s Membership Interest in the Company or purchase all of the Membership Interest of the other Member in the Company.  The Cross Offer Notice shall be given to the other Member in writing and shall set forth the proposed price and terms of payment, which price and terms will apply whether the Initiating Member sells an interest in the Company to the other Member or purchases an interest in the Company from the other Member.  The Cross Offer Notice shall be mailed to the other Member at the other Member’s address as shown on the Company’s records.



12.2	Response to Cross Offer.  The other Member shall have a period of 30 days after receiving a Cross Offer Notice to elect whether to sell his/her/its entire Membership Interest or purchase the entire Membership Interest of the Initiating Member.  This election shall be made by the other Member by giving written notice of the election to the Initiating Member.  If the other Member fails timely to give notice of its election, the other Member shall be deemed to have accepted the Initiating Member’s written Cross Offer Notice as written.



12.3 	Closing of Purchase or Sale.  The purchase or sale shall be closed within 60 days after the other Member gives notice of the other Member’s response to the Cross Offer Notice, or if no response is given, 60 days from the expiration of the period for the other Member to respond to the Cross Offer Notice.



12.4	Bona Fide Offer.  For the purposes of Section 12.1 above, a “bona fide written offer” shall mean a legally enforceable offer in writing, made and signed by an offeror or offerors who is, or who are, not an affiliate of any Member and who is a Person(s) or entity(ies) financially capable of carrying out the terms of such bona fide written offer. As used in the prior sentence, the term “affiliate,” as it relates to a Member, shall mean any parent, spouse, brother, sister, natural or adopted lineal descendant, spouse of a natural or adopted lineal descendant, sole proprietorship, partner, officer, director, employee, consultant, independent contractor, co-venturer, employer, agent, representative, creditor, or testamentary beneficiary of such Member, or any entity in which such Member owns or controls more than 5% of the issued and outstanding shares or ownership interests.



SECTION 13.	DISPOSITION OF MEMBERSHIP INTERESTS



	13.1	Voluntary Transfers Prohibited.  Except as otherwise specifically provided in this Agreement, no Member has the right to sell, assign, pledge, hypothecate, encumber, transfer, exchange, gift, bequeath or otherwise transfer for consideration or no consideration (whether or not by operation of law, except in the case of bankruptcy) all or any part of its Membership Interest (collectively a “transfer”) without the unanimous consent of the other Members.  Any attempted transfer of all or any part of a Membership Interest in violation of this Agreement will be null and void and of no effect.



	13.2	Voluntary Withdrawal.  



(a)	A Member may voluntarily withdraw from the Company in exchange for the sum of $10.00 as full and complete consideration for the Member’s Membership Interest.  A Member may exercise this right of voluntary withdrawal by tendering to the Company (i) an irrevocable assignment of his entire Membership Interest to the Company, and (i) a written demand for payment of $10.00 under this Section 13.2(a).  Such assignment and demand shall be deemed accepted upon receipt by the Company. The Company shall record the withdrawal on the books and records of the Company and tender $10.00 to such withdrawing Member within 30 days of the Company’s receipt of the assignment and demand. 



(b)	Except as set forth in Section 13.2(a) above, a Member may not voluntarily withdraw from the Company without obtaining the consent of a majority the remaining Members, which consent may be withheld for any reason or no reason at all.  In the event a withdrawal is permitted, the withdrawing Member shall tender his entire Membership Interest to the Company or the remaining Members on such terms and conditions as the remaining Members and the withdrawing Member may agree.



	13.3	Involuntary Dissociation.  



(a)	A Member shall immediately cease to be a Member on the happening of any of the following events (an “Involuntary Disassociation”; each of the following is a “Disassociation Event”):



		(i)	A Member becoming a Bankrupt Member;



(ii)	In the case of a Member that is a separate organization other than a corporation, the dissolution and commencement of winding up of the separate organization;



(iii)	In the case of a Member that is a corporation, the filing of a certificate of dissolution, or its equivalent, for the corporation or the revocation of its charter;



(iv)	In the case of a Member that is not a natural person, the sale or disposition of a majority of its voting stock or voting interest.



		(b)	In the event of an Involuntary Disassociation:



(i) 	The Member is entitled to the Book Value of such disassociating Member’s Membership Interest, reduced by any damages sustained by the Company as a result of the Member's dissociation.  Such amount shall be payable by the Company to the dissociating Member in accordance with the installment note terms set forth in Section 14.3.   



(ii) 	The “Book Value” of the disassociating Member’s Membership Interest shall be determined by the accountant regularly employed by the Company or, if there is none, an independent certified public accounting firm agreed upon by a majority of the remaining Members.  The determination made by such accountant shall be binding and conclusive upon the Company, the disassociating Member, and the remaining Members. The Book Value shall be the sum of the following:



(1) An amount equal to the disassociating Member's Capital Contributions to the Company, less any Capital Contributions returned to the disassociating Member;



(2) The amount of the profits or losses of the Company allocable to the disassociating Member for the then current fiscal year. For the purposes of this item two, such calculation shall be made assuming that the books of the Company were closed on the date of the Disassociating Event;



(3) The balance of any loan or other indebtedness owed by the Company to the disassociating Member; and



(4) Less the balance of any loan, indebtedness, or other amounts owed by the disassociating Member to the Company.



It is expressly understood that the value of goodwill is not to be considered in determining the value of the Membership Interest of such disassociating Member. 



	

SECTION 14. BUY-SELL FOR DEATH OR DISABILITY



14.1	Purchase Upon Death.  



	(a)	Purchase upon Death.	Upon the death of any Member (a “Deceased Member”) the remaining Members shall purchase, and the Deceased Member’s personal representatives shall sell, all of the Membership Interest of the Company owned by the Deceased Member at the time of the Deceased Member's death for the total amount of $1,500,000.00 (the “Deceased Purchase Price”).  



(b)	Insurance.  A Member of the Company may maintain one or more policies of life insurance upon the life of another Member for the purpose of purchasing such Member’s Membership Interest upon such Member’s death.  All such policies shall be set forth on Exhibit C to this Agreement, which is incorporated herein by this reference.  If one or more of the policies set forth on Exhibit C insuring the life of the Deceased Member are in force at the time of a Deceased Member’s death, the remaining Member shall (i) assign the remaining Member’s beneficiary interest in such policy(ies) to the Deceased Member’s personal representative, or (ii) pay the proceeds of the such life insurance policy to the Deceased Member’s personal representative within 30 days of the remaining Member’s receipt of the same or within 30 days of the appointment of such personal representative by a Court of competent jurisdiction, whichever later occurs. 



	(c)	In the event that (i) no life insurance policy set forth on Exhibit C insuring the life of the Deceased Member is in force at the time of the Deceased Member’s death or (ii) that the total death benefit of all life insurance policies insuring the life of the Deceased Member is less than $1,500,000.00, then the Remaining Members shall pay the unpaid portion of Deceased Purchase Price upon such terms and conditions as may be mutually agreed by the parties to the transaction, but in the event of failure to reach an agreement within 90 days of the appointment of the Deceased Member’s personal representative by a Court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining Members shall pay the purchase price at the closing in accordance with the terms of the installment note described in Section 14.3 below.  The closing shall occur at such time and place mutually agreeable to the parties, but in no event longer than 120 days after the appointment of the Deceased Member’s personal representative by a Court of competent jurisdiction.



14.2	Purchase Upon Disability. 



(a)	Upon the disability of a Member (a “Disabled Member”), the Company shall purchase, and such disabled Member shall sell, all of such disabled Member’s Membership Interest in the Company for a purchase price equal to the Current Value multiplied by the Disabled Member’s Membership Interest (the “Disability Purchase Price”) upon such terms and conditions as may be mutually agreed by the parties to the transaction, but if the parties cannot reach an agreement within 90 days of the date of the Current Value CPA’s (as defined below) Current Value determination report, then the Company shall pay the Disability Purchase Price at the closing in accordance with the terms of the installment note described in Section 14.3 below.  The closing shall take place at a date and time fixed by the remaining Members and the Disabled Member or his representative, but in no event longer than 120 days after the date of the Current Value CPA’s Current Value determination report.



(b)	The “disability” of a Member for purposes of this Agreement, shall mean an illness, injury or other physical or mental condition of the Member occurring for a period of forty-five (45) consecutive business days from the commencement or recurrence of such condition which results in the Member’s inability to perform during such period substantially all of the obligations the Member performed in the Member's capacity as a Member, Manager, or employee of the Company immediately prior to the commencement of such condition.  If the Company and the Member are unable to agree whether the Member is disabled within the meaning of this Subsection 14.2(b), the issue shall be submitted to and settled by binding arbitration under and pursuant to the provisions of Georgia law and the rules and regulations of the American Arbitration Association.  The parties may select any arbitrator upon whom they agree, except that if the parties are unable to agree upon an arbitrator within 30 days of the first written demand for arbitration, then the disagreement shall be considered a deadlock and resolved according to the provisions of Section 11.  While the parties shall follow the applicable rules and regulations of the American Arbitration Association, the parties are free to submit their dispute to any private arbitration program, including but not limited to the American Arbitration Association. The decision or award of the arbitrator or arbitrators in such arbitration shall be final, conclusive and binding upon each of the parties, and judgment may be entered thereon in any court of competent jurisdiction.



(c)	The “Current Value” of the Company shall be the sum of the value of all of the depreciated book value of all of the Company’s assets, minus the sum of all of the Company’s outstanding liabilities, including those liabilities that are now due or may become due in the future.  The Current Value will be determined by a written report from the certified public accountant regularly employed by the Company or, if there is none, an independent certified public accountant experienced in performing business valuations agreed upon by the remaining Members and the Disabled Member.  In the event that the remaining Members and the Disabled Member cannot agree upon a certified public accountant to determine the Current Value within 60 days of the date that the Disabled Member is determined to be disabled, then the remaining Members and the Disabled Member shall each nominate a certified public accountant, and the two nominated certified public accountants shall select a third certified public accountant to determine the Current Value.  The Managers of the Company shall provide the certified public accountant selected to determine Current Value (the “Current Value CPA”) with full and unrestricted access to the books and records of the Company for the purpose of determining Current Value.  The Current Value CPA shall submit a written Current Value determination report the Disabled Member and the Company containing his or her findings and support therefor within 60 days of such Current Value CPA’s selection.  The Current Value determination made by the Current Value CPA shall be binding and conclusive upon the Company, the remaining Members, and the Disabled Member.



14.3	Installment Payments.  If an installment note is delivered at closing, the Company or remaining Member, as appropriate, shall pay the Deceased Purchase Price or the Disability Purchase Price, as appropriate, at the closing by issuing to the Deceased Member’s personal representative, Disabled Member, or Disabled Member’s representative, as appropriate, an unsecured promissory note providing for equal quarterly installments of principal and interest over a five (5) year period at seven percent (7%) simple interest.  The first payment shall be due at closing and the second payment shall be due on the next occurring first day of January, April, July, or October.  Subsequent payments shall be due on the first day of each of such months.  

	



SECTION 15.	DISSOLUTION AND WINDING UP



	15.1	Dissolution.  The Company shall be dissolved and its affairs must be wound up upon the vote or written consent of all Members.



	15.2	Winding Up.  On dissolution of the Company, the Managers may, in the name of and on behalf of the Company, prosecute and defend suits, whether civil, criminal or administrative, sell and close the Company's business, dispose of and convey the Company's property, discharge the Company's liabilities, and distribute to the Members any remaining assets of the Company, all without affecting the liability of Members. On winding up of the Company, the assets are to be distributed as follows:



	(a)	To creditors, including any Member who is a creditor, to the extent permitted by law, in satisfaction of liabilities of the Company, whether by payment or by establishment of adequate reserves, other than liabilities for distributions to Members under the Act;



	(b)	To Members and former Members in satisfaction of liabilities for Distributions under the Act; and



	(c)	To Members first for the return of their Capital Contributions, to the extent not previously returned, and second, in accordance with their Membership Interest.



	15.3	Statement of Commencement of Winding Up.  Within thirty (30) days following the dissolution and the commencement of winding up of the Company, or at any other time there are no Members, the Managers must file a Statement of Commencement of Winding Up with the Georgia Secretary of State pursuant to the Act.



	15.4	Nonrecourse to Other Members.  Except as provided by applicable law or as expressly provided in this Agreement, on dissolution, each Member will receive a return of his, her or its Capital Contribution solely from the assets of the Company. If the assets of the Company remaining after the payment or discharge of the debts and liabilities of the Company is insufficient to return any Capital Contribution of any Member, the Member will have no recourse against any other Member.



	15.5	Termination.  On completion of the dissolution, winding up, liquidation, and distribution of the assets of the Company, the Company is deemed terminated and the Managers shall file a Certificate of Termination with the Georgia Secretary of State in accordance with the Act.



SECTION 16.	GENERAL PROVISIONS



	16.1	Notices.  Any notice, demand or other communication required or permitted to be given pursuant to this Agreement is sufficiently given for all purposes if it is in writing and (a) delivered personally to the party or to an executive officer of the party to whom the notice, demand or other communication is directed, or (b) sent by messenger, or by overnight courier, or by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, addressed to the Member, Manager, or the Company at his, her or its address set forth in this Agreement, or such address as the Member, Manager, or Company gives notice of. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, any notice is deemed given, on delivery, except if sent by registered or certified mail, then three (3) business days after the date on which it was deposited in a regularly maintained receptacle for the deposit of United States mail, addressed and sent as set forth in this section.



	16.2	Entire Agreement and Amendments.  This Agreement contains the entire agreement among the Members with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement.  No course of performance or other conduct subsequently pursued or acquiesced in, and no oral agreement or representation subsequently made, by the Members, whether or not relied or acted on, and no usage of trade, whether or not relied or acted on, amends this Agreement or impairs or otherwise affects any Member's obligations pursuant to this Agreement or any rights and remedies of a Member pursuant to this Agreement. No amendment to this Agreement is effective unless made in a writing duly executed by unanimous vote or written consent of the Members and specifically referring to each provision of this Agreement being amended.



	16.3	No Partnership Intended.  The Members have formed the Company under the Act, and expressly do not intend to form a partnership under either the Georgia Uniform Partnership Act or the Georgia Revised Uniform Limited Partnership Act. The Members do not intend to be partners to one another, or partners as to any third party. To the extent any Member, by work or action, represents to another person that any other Member is a partner or that the Company is a partnership, the Member making such wrongful representation is liable to any other Member who incurs personal liability by reason of such wrongful representation.



	16.4	Rights of Creditors and Third Parties Under Agreement.  The Agreement is entered into between the Company and the Members for the exclusive benefit of the Company, its Members, and their successors and assignees. The Agreement is expressly not intended for the benefit of any creditor of the Company or any other Person. Except and only to the extent provided by applicable statute, no such creditor or third party has any rights under the Agreement or any agreement between the Company and any Member with respect to any Capital Contribution or otherwise.



	16.5	Execution of Additional Instruments.  Each Member agrees to execute other and further statements of interests and holdings, designations, powers of attorney and other instruments necessary to comply with any laws, rules or regulations.



	16.6	Telephone Conferences.  The Members and/or Managers may participate in a meeting of Members or a meeting of the Managers, as the case may be, by means of conference telephone or similar communications equipment in which all Persons participating in the meeting can hear each other, and participation in a meeting constitutes presence of the Person at the meeting.

	

	16.7	Construction.  Whenever the singular number is used in this Agreement, and when required by the context, the same includes the plural and vice versa, and the masculine gender includes the feminine and neuter genders and vice versa.



	16.8	Waiver.  No failure of a Member to exercise, and no delay by a Member in exercising, any right or remedy under this Agreement constitutes a waiver of such right or remedy. No waiver by a Member of any right or remedy under this Agreement is effective unless made in a writing duly executed by all Members and specifically referring to each such right or remedy being waived.



	16.9	Severability.  Whenever possible, each provision of this Agreement is to be interpreted to be effective and valid under applicable law. However, if any provision of this Agreement is prohibited by or invalid under such law, it is deemed modified to conform to the minimum requirements of such law or, if for any reason it is not deemed so modified, it is prohibited or invalid only to the extent of such prohibition or invalidity without the remainder of the Agreement or any other provision being prohibited or invalid.



	16.10	Binding.  This Agreement is binding on and inures to the benefit of all Members and each of their successors and assignees.



	16.11	Counterparts.  This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original and all of which constitutes one and the same instrument.



	16.12	Governing Law.  This Agreement is governed by, and interpreted and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of Georgia, without regard to principles of conflict of laws.



	

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Members and the Company have executed this Agreement under seal and is effective on the Effective Date.
